sourceRegistry.notes: Indicator-derived Pattern Atlas rules must reference existing approved Mystery Map indicator artifacts.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries where Portuguese is an official language
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries share Portuguese as an official language, with a Lusophone Atlantic pattern plus Mozambique and Timor-Leste.

decoys.whyPlausible: The Atlantic and Latin America pattern can look colonial.
decoys.whyWrong: Brazil, Angola, Mozambique, Guinea-Bissau, and Portugal point to Portuguese, not Spanish.

decoys.whyPlausible: West and Central Africa are both visible in the set.
decoys.whyWrong: Brazil, Portugal, Mozambique, and Timor-Leste do not fit that currency pattern.

decoys.whyPlausible: Several highlighted countries face the South Atlantic.
decoys.whyWrong: Portugal and Timor-Leste break a coastline-only answer.

rules.scopeNote: Cabo Verde and Sao Tome and Principe are not present in the current 110m entity registry and are intentionally omitted.

rules.displayAnswer: South American countries where Spanish is an official language
rules.explanation: This rule highlights the Spanish-speaking mainland arc of South America, excluding Portuguese-speaking Brazil and the Guianas.

decoys.whyPlausible: Many Andean countries are Spanish-speaking and highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Argentina, Paraguay, Uruguay, and Venezuela make the set wider than the Andes.

decoys.whyPlausible: Argentina, Paraguay, and Uruguay are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Brazil is a founding member but is not highlighted, while many highlighted countries are not founding members.

decoys.whyPlausible: Colombia and Ecuador are included.
decoys.whyWrong: Most highlighted countries are south of the Equator, and Brazil is missing.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped West African countries where French is an official language
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries form a mostly contiguous Francophone West African pattern from Senegal and Guinea through the Sahel and Gulf of Guinea.

decoys.whyPlausible: All highlighted countries are in West Africa.
decoys.whyWrong: English-speaking ECOWAS countries such as Ghana and Nigeria are not highlighted.

decoys.whyPlausible: Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger are central to the pattern.
decoys.whyWrong: Benin, Cote d'Ivoire, Guinea, Senegal, and Togo make the set broader than the Sahel.

decoys.whyPlausible: Several highlighted countries use a CFA franc currency.
decoys.whyWrong: Guinea is highlighted but does not use the CFA franc.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries where German is an official language
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries share official German status, producing a compact Central European cluster plus multilingual Belgium and Luxembourg.

decoys.whyPlausible: Most highlighted countries are Germany or neighbors of Germany.
decoys.whyWrong: Denmark, Poland, Czechia, France, and the Netherlands border Germany but are not highlighted.

decoys.whyPlausible: Belgium, Germany, and Luxembourg are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Italy, France, and the Netherlands are founding members but are not highlighted.

decoys.whyPlausible: Austria and Switzerland are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Belgium and Luxembourg are not Alpine countries.

rules.scopeNote: Liechtenstein is not present in the current 110m entity registry and is intentionally omitted.

rules.displayAnswer: Landlocked countries in South America
rules.explanation: Bolivia and Paraguay are the two South American countries without a coastline.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries crossed by the Tropic of Capricorn
decoys.whyPlausible: Both highlighted countries are crossed by or near the tropic.
decoys.whyWrong: Argentina, Brazil, Chile, and others also fit that tropic pattern.

decoys.displayAnswer: Former Spanish colonies in South America
decoys.whyPlausible: Both highlighted countries are Spanish-speaking South American states.
decoys.whyWrong: That answer would include most of Spanish-speaking South America.

decoys.whyPlausible: Bolivia is strongly Andean.
decoys.whyWrong: Paraguay is not an Andean country, and several Andean countries are missing.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries with a Mediterranean Sea coastline
rules.explanation: The highlighted set wraps the northern, eastern, and southern Mediterranean coastline.

decoys.displayAnswer: European Union members on the Mediterranean
decoys.whyPlausible: Several highlighted European coasts are in the EU.
decoys.whyWrong: Albania, Algeria, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Egypt, Lebanon, Libya, Montenegro, Morocco, Syria, Tunisia, and Turkey are not EU members.

decoys.whyPlausible: Turkey is highlighted and sits near both basins.
decoys.whyWrong: Most highlighted countries are on the Mediterranean rather than the Black Sea.

decoys.whyPlausible: The southern Mediterranean edge is prominent.
decoys.whyWrong: Southern Europe and the Levant are also highlighted.

rules.scopeNote: Malta is not present in the current 110m entity registry and is intentionally omitted.

rules.displayAnswer: Countries with a Caspian Sea coastline
rules.explanation: These five countries border the enclosed Caspian Sea.

decoys.displayAnswer: Former Soviet republics in Central Asia
decoys.whyPlausible: Kazakhstan and Turkmenistan are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Iran is highlighted, Russia is not a Central Asian republic, and Uzbekistan is missing.

decoys.whyPlausible: Russia is highlighted and the region is nearby.
decoys.whyWrong: Azerbaijan, Iran, Kazakhstan, and Turkmenistan do not border the Black Sea.

decoys.whyPlausible: Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, and Turkmenistan are often treated as landlocked despite the Caspian coast.
decoys.whyWrong: Russia and Iran are not landlocked, and many Asian landlocked countries are missing.

rules.displayAnswer: Countries that border Germany
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries are Germany's nine land neighbors.

decoys.whyPlausible: Belgium, France, Luxembourg, and the Netherlands are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Austria, Czechia, Denmark, Poland, and Switzerland are not founding members.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries where German is an official language
decoys.whyPlausible: Austria, Belgium, Luxembourg, and Switzerland are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Czechia, Denmark, France, the Netherlands, and Poland are border neighbors, not German-official countries.

decoys.whyPlausible: Many highlighted countries participate in Schengen.
decoys.whyWrong: The Schengen Area contains many more countries and is not centered only on Germany's border.

rules.displayAnswer: Countries that border China
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries are China's land neighbors, spanning Central, South, Southeast, and Northeast Asia.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries in the Belt and Road Initiative
decoys.whyPlausible: The geography clusters around China.
decoys.whyWrong: That initiative is far broader and would include many countries outside this border ring.

decoys.whyPlausible: Several highlighted neighbors are landlocked.
decoys.whyWrong: India, Myanmar, North Korea, Pakistan, Russia, and Vietnam have coastlines.

decoys.whyPlausible: Laos, Myanmar, and Vietnam are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: The set extends far beyond Southeast Asia into Central Asia, South Asia, and Russia.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries crossed by the Equator
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries sit along the Equator in South America, Central Africa, East Africa, and Indonesia.

decoys.whyPlausible: Every highlighted country is tropical or near tropical latitudes.
decoys.whyWrong: The tropics include a much larger belt; this rule is the Equator itself.

decoys.whyPlausible: Brazil, Colombia, and Ecuador are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: African and Asian Equator countries are highlighted too, while Peru is not.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries on the Gulf of Guinea
decoys.whyPlausible: Gabon and Congo are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: The pattern crosses continents and is not a coastline rule.

rules.scopeNote: Small Equator-crossed states not present in the current 110m entity registry are intentionally omitted.

rules.displayAnswer: Countries crossed by the Tropic of Capricorn
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries follow the southern tropic across South America, southern Africa, Madagascar, and Australia.

decoys.whyPlausible: The highlighted countries are mostly south of the Equator.
decoys.whyWrong: Many Southern Hemisphere countries are not crossed by this latitude.

decoys.whyPlausible: Australia, Namibia, Botswana, and Chile can suggest desert geography.
decoys.whyWrong: Brazil, Paraguay, Mozambique, and Madagascar point to a latitude line instead.

decoys.whyPlausible: Botswana, Mozambique, Namibia, and South Africa are clustered.
decoys.whyWrong: The South American and Australian highlights make the rule global.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries and territories crossed by the Arctic Circle
rules.explanation: The highlighted set follows the Arctic Circle across North America, Greenland, the Nordic region, and Russia.

decoys.whyPlausible: Finland, Iceland, Norway, and Sweden are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Canada, Greenland, Russia, and the United States are also highlighted, while Denmark is not highlighted as a separate mainland country.

decoys.whyPlausible: Many highlighted countries have Arctic coasts.
decoys.whyWrong: Finland and Sweden do not border the Arctic Ocean directly.

decoys.whyPlausible: The latitude pattern suggests tundra.
decoys.whyWrong: Climate zones are broader and less exact than the Arctic Circle line.

rules.scopeNote: Greenland is present as a mapped entity in the current registry.

rules.displayAnswer: Countries containing part of the Sahara Desert
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries span the Sahara from the Atlantic edge across North Africa into Sudan and Chad.

decoys.whyPlausible: Several highlighted countries are Arab League members.
decoys.whyWrong: Chad, Mali, and Niger are highlighted because of the Sahara, not Arab League membership.

decoys.whyPlausible: Mali, Niger, Chad, and Sudan are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Algeria, Egypt, Libya, Morocco, and Tunisia point north to the Sahara itself.

decoys.whyPlausible: Several North African Mediterranean countries are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Mali, Mauritania, Niger, Sudan, and Chad have no Mediterranean coast.

rules.displayAnswer: Countries of the Greater Antilles
rules.explanation: Cuba, Jamaica, Haiti, and the Dominican Republic occupy the major island group known as the Greater Antilles.

decoys.whyPlausible: All highlighted countries are in the Caribbean.
decoys.whyWrong: That wider answer would include many smaller island states and The Bahamas.

decoys.whyPlausible: Cuba and the Dominican Republic are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Haiti and Jamaica do not fit that language answer.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries bordering the Caribbean Sea in mainland America
decoys.whyPlausible: The highlights sit in the Caribbean basin.
decoys.whyWrong: These are island countries, not mainland coastal countries.

rules.displayAnswer: Countries containing part of the Andes
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries trace the Andes along western South America and into Venezuela.

decoys.whyPlausible: Every highlighted country is Spanish-speaking.
decoys.whyWrong: Paraguay and Uruguay are Spanish-speaking South American countries but are not Andean.

decoys.displayAnswer: Pacific coast countries in South America
decoys.whyPlausible: Chile, Peru, Ecuador, and Colombia are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Bolivia and Venezuela are highlighted, while the rule is mountain geography rather than coast.

decoys.whyPlausible: Several highlighted countries include Amazon basin territory.
decoys.whyWrong: Chile and Argentina point to the Andes, not the Amazon.

rules.displayAnswer: Countries the Mekong River flows through
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries follow the Mekong from China through mainland Southeast Asia to Vietnam.

decoys.whyPlausible: Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand, and Vietnam are ASEAN members.
decoys.whyWrong: China is highlighted but is not an ASEAN member, while several ASEAN members are missing.

decoys.whyPlausible: China, Laos, Myanmar, and Vietnam are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Cambodia and Thailand do not border China, and many Chinese neighbors are missing.

decoys.whyPlausible: Most highlighted countries are in mainland Southeast Asia.
decoys.whyWrong: The river-source clue adds China and excludes Malaysia.

rules.displayAnswer: Countries containing part of the Amazon rainforest
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries contain Amazon rainforest territory in northern and central South America.

decoys.whyPlausible: Brazil, Colombia, and Ecuador are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Bolivia, Guyana, Peru, Suriname, and Venezuela are highlighted without being Equator-crossed on this map.

decoys.whyPlausible: Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Brazil, Guyana, and Suriname point to rainforest-basin geography instead.

decoys.whyPlausible: Brazil is highlighted and the pattern is South American.
decoys.whyWrong: Most highlighted countries are not founding MERCOSUR members, and Argentina, Paraguay, and Uruguay are missing.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped ASEAN member countries
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries are ASEAN members represented in the current country registry.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries the Mekong River flows through
decoys.whyPlausible: Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand, and Vietnam are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, and the Philippines are ASEAN members outside the Mekong route.

decoys.whyPlausible: Laos, Myanmar, and Vietnam are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Most ASEAN members do not border China, and many Chinese neighbors are missing.

decoys.whyPlausible: Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, and the Philippines are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand, and Vietnam are mainland ASEAN members.

rules.scopeNote: Singapore is not present in the current 110m entity registry and is intentionally omitted.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped Gulf Cooperation Council members
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries are GCC members present in the current map registry.

decoys.whyPlausible: The highlights cluster on and around the peninsula.
decoys.whyWrong: Yemen is on the peninsula but is not highlighted, and the rule is organization membership.

decoys.whyPlausible: Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and the United Arab Emirates are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Qatar and Oman are highlighted here because of GCC membership, not current OPEC membership.

decoys.whyPlausible: Several highlighted countries have Persian Gulf coastlines.
decoys.whyWrong: Iran and Iraq have Persian Gulf coastlines but are not GCC members.

rules.scopeNote: Bahrain is not present in the current 110m entity registry and is intentionally omitted.

rules.displayAnswer: Nordic Council sovereign member countries
rules.explanation: Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, and Sweden are the sovereign Nordic Council countries.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries crossed by the Arctic Circle
decoys.whyPlausible: Finland, Iceland, Norway, and Sweden are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Denmark is highlighted by membership, while Canada, Russia, and the United States are not Nordic Council countries.

decoys.whyPlausible: Denmark, Finland, and Sweden are Baltic-facing.
decoys.whyWrong: Iceland and Norway break a Baltic-only pattern.

decoys.displayAnswer: European Union members in Northern Europe
decoys.whyPlausible: Denmark, Finland, and Sweden are EU members.
decoys.whyWrong: Iceland and Norway are highlighted but are not EU members.

rules.displayAnswer: Pacific Alliance member countries
rules.explanation: Chile, Colombia, Mexico, and Peru are the Pacific Alliance members.

decoys.displayAnswer: Pacific coast countries in Latin America
decoys.whyPlausible: The highlights all have Pacific-facing geography.
decoys.whyWrong: That coastline answer would include additional countries such as Ecuador and parts of Central America.

decoys.whyPlausible: Chile, Colombia, and Peru are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Mexico is not in South America, and many Spanish-speaking South American countries are missing.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries containing part of the Andes
decoys.whyPlausible: Chile, Colombia, and Peru are Andean.
decoys.whyWrong: Mexico is highlighted because of organization membership, not Andes geography.

rules.displayAnswer: G7 member countries
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries are the Group of Seven members.

decoys.whyPlausible: France, the United Kingdom, and the United States are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Canada, Germany, Italy, and Japan are highlighted, while China and Russia are missing.

decoys.whyPlausible: Several highlighted countries are NATO founders.
decoys.whyWrong: Japan is highlighted but is not in NATO, and many NATO founders are missing.

decoys.whyPlausible: The set contains several very large economies.
decoys.whyWrong: The exact seven-country set is the G7 membership list, not a current GDP ranking.

rules.displayAnswer: European Union founding member countries
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries are the six founding states of the European integration project that became the EU.

decoys.whyPlausible: These countries use the euro and were early European integration members.
decoys.whyWrong: The answer is EU founding membership; it is not all original euro adopters.

decoys.whyPlausible: Belgium, France, Luxembourg, and the Netherlands border Germany.
decoys.whyWrong: Italy is highlighted and several German neighbors are missing.

decoys.whyPlausible: Belgium, Luxembourg, and the Netherlands are all highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: France, Germany, and Italy make it the EU founding-six pattern.

rules.displayAnswer: MERCOSUR founding member countries
rules.explanation: Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay founded MERCOSUR under the Treaty of Asuncion.

decoys.whyPlausible: Argentina, Paraguay, and Uruguay are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Brazil is highlighted and many Spanish-speaking countries are missing.

decoys.whyPlausible: Paraguay is highlighted and central.
decoys.whyWrong: Bolivia is not highlighted, while Argentina, Brazil, and Uruguay have coastlines.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries crossed by the Tropic of Capricorn
decoys.whyPlausible: Argentina, Brazil, and Paraguay are crossed by the tropic.
decoys.whyWrong: Uruguay is highlighted by MERCOSUR founding membership, and several tropic-crossed countries are missing.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped euro area member countries
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries are euro area members represented in the current entity registry.

decoys.whyPlausible: Every highlighted country is an EU member.
decoys.whyWrong: EU members that do not use the euro, such as Denmark and Poland, are not highlighted.

decoys.whyPlausible: The map overlaps heavily with Schengen geography.
decoys.whyWrong: Cyprus is highlighted for euro membership, and some Schengen countries outside the euro area are missing.

decoys.displayAnswer: EU founding members and later joiners before 2004
decoys.whyPlausible: Many early EU economies are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Baltic countries, Slovakia, Slovenia, Croatia, and Cyprus show this is currency membership.

rules.scopeNote: Malta is not present in the current 110m entity registry and is intentionally omitted.

rules.displayAnswer: Countries using a CFA franc currency
rules.explanation: The highlighted countries use either the West African CFA franc or the Central African CFA franc.

decoys.displayAnswer: Mapped West African countries where French is official
decoys.whyPlausible: Many highlighted countries are Francophone West African states.
decoys.whyWrong: Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Congo, Equatorial Guinea, and Gabon are Central African, while Guinea is not highlighted.

decoys.whyPlausible: Several West African highlighted countries are ECOWAS members.
decoys.whyWrong: Central African CFA users are highlighted too, and Ghana and Nigeria are missing.

decoys.whyPlausible: The colonial-history signal is tempting.
decoys.whyWrong: Guinea is a former French colony but is not highlighted, and Equatorial Guinea is highlighted through the currency union.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries in the top quartile for renewable electricity share
rules.explanation: This rule highlights mapped countries in the highest quartile of the approved Mystery Map renewable electricity share indicator.

decoys.displayAnswer: Top quartile for forest area share
decoys.whyPlausible: Hydropower and forested countries overlap in some regions.
decoys.whyWrong: Countries such as Lesotho, Tajikistan, and Uruguay point to electricity mix rather than forest cover.

decoys.whyPlausible: Some clean-grid countries also have low air pollution.
decoys.whyWrong: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, and other air-quality patterns do not match this renewable-electricity set.

decoys.whyPlausible: Hydropower explains many high renewable-electricity shares.
decoys.whyWrong: The source indicator is all renewable electricity, not a hydropower-only rule.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries in the top quartile for forest area share
rules.explanation: This rule highlights mapped countries in the highest quartile of the approved Mystery Map forest area share indicator.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries containing part of the Amazon rainforest
decoys.whyPlausible: Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Peru, Suriname, and Venezuela are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: The set also includes boreal, island, Southeast Asian, and Central African forest countries.

decoys.displayAnswer: Top quartile for renewable electricity share
decoys.whyPlausible: Several forested countries also have hydro-heavy power systems.
decoys.whyWrong: Japan, South Korea, and Brunei are forest-share clues, not renewable-electricity leaders.

decoys.whyPlausible: Many tropical forest countries near the Equator are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Finland, Sweden, Japan, Latvia, and Russia make this a forest-cover indicator pattern.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries in the bottom quartile for PM2.5 exposure
rules.explanation: This rule highlights mapped countries in the lowest quartile of the approved Mystery Map PM2.5 exposure indicator.

decoys.whyPlausible: Many low-PM2.5 countries are high-income and connected.
decoys.whyWrong: The set includes low-pollution island and sparsely populated countries that are not simply the internet-use top quartile.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries crossed by the Arctic Circle
decoys.whyPlausible: Greenland, Finland, Iceland, Sweden, Norway, Canada, the United States, and Russia are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Many non-Arctic countries are highlighted because this is an air-pollution indicator.

decoys.displayAnswer: Top quartile for forest area share
decoys.whyPlausible: Forested and sparsely populated countries overlap with low PM2.5 exposure.
decoys.whyWrong: Countries such as Belgium, the Netherlands, and Germany are not forest-share leaders.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries in the top quartile for internet users
rules.explanation: This rule highlights mapped countries in the highest quartile of the approved Mystery Map internet users indicator.

decoys.displayAnswer: Top quartile for GDP per capita
decoys.whyPlausible: High-income countries are prominent.
decoys.whyWrong: Jordan, Belarus, Kyrgyzstan, China, and Uruguay point to internet-use saturation rather than income alone.

decoys.whyPlausible: Canada, Germany, the United Kingdom, and the United States are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: France, Italy, and Japan are not highlighted here, while many non-G7 countries are.

decoys.whyPlausible: Several euro area countries are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Gulf states, Korea, Australia, Canada, China, and others show this is a connectivity indicator.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries in the top quartile for water stress
rules.explanation: This rule highlights mapped countries in the highest quartile of the approved Mystery Map water stress indicator.

decoys.displayAnswer: Countries containing part of the Sahara Desert
decoys.whyPlausible: North African desert countries are prominent.
decoys.whyWrong: The set also includes South Asia, Central Asia, East Asia, Europe, and the Caribbean.

decoys.displayAnswer: Top quartile for fossil electricity share
decoys.whyPlausible: Several Gulf and North African countries have fossil-heavy power systems.
decoys.whyWrong: The rule follows water withdrawals relative to resources, not electricity generation mix.

decoys.whyPlausible: Mediterranean countries such as Libya, Egypt, Syria, Tunisia, Lebanon, Morocco, Turkey, and Spain appear.
decoys.whyWrong: The map includes many non-Mediterranean dry or heavily withdrawing countries.

rules.displayAnswer: Mapped countries in the top quartile for tourism receipts as a share of exports
rules.explanation: This rule highlights mapped countries in the highest quartile of the approved Mystery Map tourism receipts share indicator.

decoys.whyPlausible: Vanuatu, The Bahamas, Timor-Leste, Fiji, Dominican Republic, Haiti, Sri Lanka, Madagascar, New Zealand, and Cyprus are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: The set also includes mainland tourism-dependent economies across Europe, Africa, and Asia.

decoys.whyPlausible: Albania, Montenegro, Lebanon, Croatia, Greece, Egypt, Morocco, and Cyprus are highlighted.
decoys.whyWrong: Many highlighted countries are outside the Mediterranean basin.

decoys.displayAnswer: Top quartile for international tourism arrivals
decoys.whyPlausible: Tourism geography is the common theme.
decoys.whyWrong: This is receipts as a share of exports, so smaller tourism-dependent economies can outrank raw-arrival giants.
